task.domains: (topic task) (topic hi-d) (topic ui-d) (topic sw-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic ut-d) a(base ishcondition) a(base varid) a(base varref) a(base cid) a(base partslist) (topic oce-d) (topic sdlVariables-c) (topic oceCommonElements-c) (topic oceMetaDecl-c) (topic oceTopic-c) (topic oceTopic-c task oceTask-c) (topic oceTopic-c hi-d oceHighlight-c) (topic oceTopic-c ui-d oceUi-c) (topic oceTopic-c sw-d oceSoftware-c)
task.title: 12 Load media from the jumbo roll

abstract.p: You can find a description on how to use the jumbo roll in the separate user manual of the Fotoba jumbo roll. It describes amongst others:

li.p: how to align the media with the printer,
how to spool media to make a blouse.

abstract.p: In this document you will find information on how to load the media from the jumbo roll into the system.
This procedure is also explained in a separate instruction video, click here to visit.

li.p: Calibration can be performed on the media loaded on the jumbo roll.
Load the media roll centered onto the core on the jumbo roll.
On the control panel of the jumbo roll select Manual transport.
Load the media for outside printing is preferred.
Print quality is guaranteed after 1 meter (39.4 inch).
When the media is taped to the take-up roll.

note.p: Do not use the manual transport of media of the jumbo roll when the media is loaded on the printer or when the printer is in an error state.

step.cmd: Place a marker on the frame of the media input unit to identify the width of the media (for example a piece of tape).

note.p: The notch in the illustration above indicates the center of the guiding plate of the media input unit. Place the marker at a distance "media width divided by 2" from the center notch.

step.cmd: Spool 21 cm (8.3 inch) of the media beyond the guiding plate (just before the last roller) of the Jumbo roll.

Pull the media tight towards the roll and add a mark (line) at a distance of 21 cm (8.3 inch) from the leading edge. Do this on both sides of the media, left and right. You can use the guiding plate of the jumbo roll for reference.

note.p: When you load a media with a leading edge that is not completely straight, measure like shown in the illustration below.
Make sure that the difference between left and right edge of the media is not be too much to prevent that the printer prints directly on the platen.

step.cmd: Spool the media further. Stop just before the media touches the floor.
Before you feed the media into the printer, align the media with the marker on the printer (the tape you applied in step 1).

note.p: It is highly recommended to perform the next two steps with two persons.

step.cmd: Align the 2 markers (lines) on the left and right side of the media with the input unit, see illustration. Distance to red dotted line is 3 cm (1.18 inch).

Feed the media into the media input unit. The input unit pinch will start to rotate for 2 seconds and grab the media. Check the media position at the reference marker (lines) position. The allowed tolerance is ± 5 mm (0.19 inch) at the left and ± 5 mm (0.19 inch) at the right side.

info.p: If the position is not within the tolerance is ± 5 mm (0.19 inch), pull the media back (2 persons preferred) and feed again until the position is within the tolerance.

step.cmd: Push the jumbo roll unwinder towards the printer on both sides and lock the unit.
Lower the buffer unit and spool media. See illustration below.

info.p: After this, go to the control panel of the jumbo roll and select Automatic transport and choose the required rotating direction.

postreq.p: On the control panel of the printer select the External roll for your print job from the jumbo roll.
